CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-12-2012 Continued To Nov-28-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-07-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: lorna walker, esq., law offices of sweet & walker, p.c., 415.334.1600, for plaintiff cobe color cosmetics, inc.
dean christopherson, esq., dawe & christopherson, 925.256.6677, for defendant wells fargo bank.
peter j. marshall, esq., palmer, lombardi & donohue, 415.489.4801, for defendant preferred bank.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT PREFERRED BANK, A CORPORATION's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Denied. The face of the First Amended Complaint does not clearly and affirmatively show that plaintiff's claims are time-barred. (counsel for plaintiff to prepare a form of order mirroring the court's ruling and email to court- copying opposing counsel- for signature). Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-07-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: lorna walker, esq., law offices of sweet & walker, p.c., 415.334.1600, for plaintiff cobe color cosmetics, inc.
dean christopherson, esq., dawe & christopherson, 925.256.6677, for defendant wells fargo bank.
peter j. marshall, esq., palmer, lombardi & donohue, 415.489.4801, for defendant preferred bank.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT PREFERRED BANK, A CORPORATION's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action, sustained with fifteen days leave to amend as to the third cause of action, and overruled as to the claim for unauthorized payment of check. The face of the First Amended Complaint does not clearly and affirmatively show that any of plaintiffs' claims are time-barred. The first cause of action is preempted by the California Commercial Code. It is unlikely that leave to amend can cure the defect since the third cause of action already alleges violations of the applicable California Commercial Code section. The third cause of action, however, fails to allege Defendant Preferred Bank's duty to Plaintiff. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to cure the defect. The claim for unauthorized payment of check is adequately pled. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-07-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: lorna walker, esq., law offices of sweet & walker, p.c., 415.334.1600, for plaintiff cobe color cosmetics, inc.
dean christopherson, esq., dawe & christopherson, 925.256.6677, for defendant wells fargo bank.
peter j. marshall, esq., palmer, lombardi & donohue, 415.489.4801, for defendant preferred bank.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained without leave to amend as to the first and fourth causes of action and sustained with fifteen days leave to amend as to the second cause of action. The face of the First Amended Complaint does not clearly and affirmatively show that plaintiff's claims are time-barred. The first and fourth causes of action are preempted by the California Commercial Code. It is unlikely leave to amend can cure this defect since the second cause of action already alleges violations of the applicable California Commercial Code section. The second cause of action, however, fails to allege Defendant Wells Fargo Bank's duty to Plaintiff. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to cure the defect. The Court declines to take judicial notice of the requested documents. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.
